change_summary: Shadow-audited sector-leadership relative-strength trend-continuation candidates across three fixed OHLCV windows without changing production strategy code.
hypothesis: A sector-leadership relative-strength trend-continuation shadow entry may surface replayable candidates with useful forward returns and limited same-day A/B overlap, distinct from rejected macro-defensive static sectors and recent opening-range/gap/VCP shadow variants.
notes: Artifact: docs/experiments/artifacts/exp_20260426_sector_leadership_trend_shadow.json. Candidate counts were 381/843/511 across late_strong/mid_weak/old_thin. Same-day A/B overlap was 16.27%/14.47%/17.61%. 10d forward return was mixed: late_strong -0.92%, mid_weak +4.11%, old_thin +2.03%. Decision: observed_only; not production-promotable because dominant recent window is negative and candidate count is broad, not scarce.

locked_variables: sector leadership relative-strength trend-continuation shadow entry definition
parameters.single_causal_variable: sector leadership relative-strength trend-continuation shadow entry
